python3 -c "
import csv
from pathlib import Path
# Sample a few datasets to understand structure
datasets = [
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/insulin/Fits',
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/lysozyme/Fits',
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/Abeta/Cohen2013',
]
for d in datasets:
    p = Path(d)
    if p.exists():
        for f in p.iterdir():
            if f.name.endswith('.tsv'):
                with open(f) as fh:
                    reader = csv.reader(fh, delimiter='\t')
                    header = next(reader)
                    rows = [next(reader) for _ in range(3) if True]
                print(f'{f.name}: cols={header[:5]}, first_rows={[r[:3] for r in rows[:2]]}')
"

python3 -c "
import csv
from pathlib import Path

datasets_to_check = [
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/insulin/Fits/Nielsen2001_seeded_sec/fit.tsv',
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/lysozyme/Fits/Hasecke2018_7uM_sec/fit.tsv',
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/Abeta/Cohen2013/Ab42_sec_IN_HOURS/fit.tsv',
]
for path in datasets_to_check:
    p = Path(path)
    if p.exists():
        with open(p) as f:
            reader = csv.reader(f, delimiter='\t')
            header = next(reader)
            rows = list(reader)
        print(f'Dataset: {p.parent.name}')
        print(f'  Header: {header}')
        print(f'  Rows: {len(rows)}, Cols: {len(header)}')
        print(f'  First row: {rows[0]}')
        print(f'  Last row: {rows[-1]}')
        print()
"
